statusLine: EU fuel security remains under strain with 16 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, though aggregate stocks stay above minimum thresholds.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure despite maintaining adequate aggregate reserves, with EU-wide stocks at 112.8 days for petrol, 101.5 days for diesel, and 95.1 days for jet fuel—all above the 90-day mandatory minimum. However, the distribution across member states reveals concerning vulnerabilities, with 16 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel category.

Romania presents the most severe risk profile with all three fuel types in critical status: petrol at just 40.4 days, diesel at 30.2 days, and jet fuel at 76.4 days. Poland and Luxembourg also face multi-fuel critical shortages. Jet fuel emerges as the most constrained fuel type across the bloc, with 17 of 27 countries below the 90-day threshold, including major economies like Germany (64.7 days), France (67.5 days), and Spain (42.1 days).

The fuel supply pressure coincides with elevated oil prices, with Brent crude at $105.98 per barrel—a 46.2% increase since late February 2026 following Middle East supply disruptions. This has translated to high pump prices across the EU, averaging €1.817 per liter for petrol and €1.897 for diesel, with Netherlands and Denmark experiencing the steepest costs above €2.30 per liter for petrol.

While several countries maintain comfortable reserves—particularly Estonia and Slovenia with exceptionally high jet fuel stocks at 365 days—the uneven distribution of stocks and concentration of critical shortages in multiple member states suggests the EU's fuel security remains under significant strain despite meeting aggregate targets.
keyPoints: 16 of 27 EU countries hold critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with Romania facing the most severe shortages across all fuel types
Jet fuel shows the greatest vulnerability with 17 of 27 countries below the 90-day minimum threshold, affecting major economies including Germany and France
Oil prices have surged 46.2% since late February to $105.98/barrel due to Middle East disruptions, driving EU pump prices to €1.817/L for petrol
Despite individual country shortages, EU aggregate stocks remain above mandatory minimums at 112.8 days petrol, 101.5 days diesel, and 95.1 days jet fuel
Strategic reserve releases following supply disruptions have contributed to the uneven distribution of stocks across member states
